variables.sim_top1: Highest vector similarity score between incoming email and retrieved examples
variables.sim_top2: Second-highest vector similarity score between incoming email and retrieved examples
variables.rerank_score: Cross-check score from reranking step for the best candidate

weight_rationale.sim_top1: 0.50 gives strongest influence to primary semantic match because it is the most stable signal across noisy emails.
weight_rationale.margin_term: 0.30 on (sim_top1-sim_top2) penalizes ambiguous cases where two categories look similarly close.
weight_rationale.rerank_score: 0.20 adds extra quality control from reranking without overpowering base retrieval signals.

formula.why_not_040_sim_top1: Lowering sim_top1 from 0.50 to 0.40 makes the final score more sensitive to margin/rerank noise and can over-escalate clear matches. 0.50 is chosen as a balanced default before calibration.
formula.calibration_note: Run grid search on labeled validation data to tune weights and thresholds after collecting more reviewed emails.
